THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF MASSA, ANTONIO BULLOCK, FRED RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT VERNIC, FRANJO SF REHABITAT INC. $2,500.00 PRINCIPAL, $110.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,610.00 STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
CASE_TRANSFERRED TO SUPERIOR COURT, RECORD AND EXHIBITS TRANSFERRED

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT MASSA, ANTONIO BULLOCK, FRED RECOVER FROM APPELLANT SF REHABITAT INC. $1,700.00 PRINCIPAL, $110.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $1,810.00, ***** NOTE: FRANJO VERNIC IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE BY THE COURT. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PROVE OWNERSHIP OF THE FENCE. SF REHABITAT INC. IS LEGALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR OVERSPRAYING, DAMAGE TO LEMON TREE, LOW PRESSURE IRRIGATION SYSTEM, DAMAGE TO CEMENT RAILING ON REAR STEPS, WHICH IN ABSENCE OF SPECIFIC EVIDENCE IS ESTIMATED BY THE COURT AT $1,700.00. calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 11:03am
Judge Pro Tem Elliott Myles, nothing on record, clerk-beth Wilber, Dept. 525: Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Respondents, Antonio Massa & Frederik Bullock, appear in Pro Per; Appellant, Franjo Vernic, appears in Pro Per. Appearing with Respondent is a witness: Alan Calaghan. Parties, previously sworn, present respectively, their cases in the narrative and respond to questions from Judge Myles. Franco Venic is dismissed with prejudice by the Court.
11:59am
Matter is submitted. No new evidence is marked or retained.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Dec-28-2009 Judgment Entered
